LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE NOV-23-2020 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT NEXANT, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF KAREN HENNESSEY DOES NOT OPPOSE DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE FIRST AND SECOND CAUSES OF ACTION OF THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, AND ACCORDINGLY THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO HER CLAIMS. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE CLAIMS ASSERTED IN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT BY PLAINTIFF KIM PAGE. ORDER SIGNED. (SEE SIGNED ORDER FOR COMPLETE RULING.) (See Word minutes for complete ruling.)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/EPS)

DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-23-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT NEXANT, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Plaintiff Karen Hennessey does not oppose Defendant's demurrer to the first and second causes of action of the First Amended Complaint, and accordingly the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to her claims. The demurrer is sustained with 20 days leave to amend as to the claims asserted in the First Amended Complaint by Plaintiff Kim Page. (See Word minutes for complete ruling.)

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/EPS)
